name: Chard-Wrapped Salmon and Duxelles
recipeIngredient: 1/3 lb Mushrooms, commercial, wild, or a mixture of both
4 large Swiss Chard Leaves, stemmed and blanched, rinsed in cold water, drained and patted dry
1/2 cup Brown Veal or Poultry Stock
recipeInstructions:
1. Finely dice the mushrooms (if using wild mushrooms with an interesting shape, cut them into long strips or wedges).
2. Melt 1 tablespoon of butter in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat.
3. Add the mushrooms and onion to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until the mushrooms begin to wilt.
4. Add garlic, savory, pepper, and salt. Cook until the liquid given off by the mushrooms is nearly gone.
5. Add sherry and cook until evaporated. Remove from heat, taste for seasoning, and set aside to cool.
6. Remove any pin bones from the salmon fillets and season lightly with remaining salt and pepper.
7. Lay out 2 chard leaves in opposite directions, slightly overlapping.
8. Place a salmon fillet across the middle, skin side up. If the belly flap is especially long or thin, fold it double for a more compact, even shape.
9. Arrange half of the mushroom mixture on each side of the salmon.
10. Fold the near end of the leaves over the fish, tucking in the sides, then fold the whole thing over the remaining leaf, forming an envelope.
11. Trim off the remainder of the leaf to form a neat package. Repeat with the remaining salmon fillet.
12. Heat stock and wine to a simmer in the same skillet used for the mushrooms.
13. Place the salmon packages in the pan, seam side down, cover, and cook at a simmer until a skewer easily enters the center of the fish (8-12 minutes depending on thickness).
14. Transfer the fish to plates. Turn off the heat under the skillet and reduce the stock slightly.
15. Swirl in the remaining butter, taste for seasoning, and pour over the fish.
Per Serving: Calories: 415, Protein: 32 g, Carbohydrate: 9 g, Fat: 27 g, Saturated Fat: 11 g, Cholesterol: 125 mg, Sodium: 495 mg, Fiber: 2 g.
